Document:
# Elves
Elves are hard to distinguish from humans for most. They can usually be spotted by their pointed ears, though the effect is sometimes not obvious.
## Damage The body of an Elf is attuned to magic, not bruality. As a result they take [color=#ffa500]10% more damage from Blunt[/color] but [color=#1e90ff]25% less damage from Poison and Cellular decay[/color].
## Origins
Elves can come from a variety of worlds and have many different cultural backgrounds, but are usually lumped into three broad categories: High Elves, who aspire to moral goodness; Dark Elves, who are ruthlessly pragmatic if not downright evil; and Wood Elves, who prize their forest homes above any particular ideology.
The native language of the Elves is commonly called Aielic, though it may have different names on different planets. Most Elves can also understand Sylvan (the language of Dionae and other treefolk) and Darktongue (the language of evil creatures.) Unlike Dwarves, they are not usually familiar with Sol Common.
It is rumored that there is an organization of Elves somewhere in distant space, called MAGE, which opposes the reckless showboating of the Human-run Wizarding Federation, but whether this organization is real or has any actual power is a matter of pure speculation.
